title: Erigeron spathulifolius
taxonomy: Erigeron spathulifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), genus Erigeron. This annual or perennial plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Erigeron spathulifolius is further classified as a member of the subgenus Erigeron and section Proliferifolii. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Erigeron spathulifolius is closely related to other species in the same genus, such as Erigeron glaucus and Erigeron breweri.
uses: Erigeron spathulifolius, also known as a dune fleabane or dune daisy, is a wildflower native to California in the United States.  Native American tribes have traditionally used this plant for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including colds, sore throats, and skin irritations. The leaves and flowers of Erigeron spathulifolius contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant and its potential health benefits.
